headline: Roy Anderson Corp Awarded $114 Million Jones Hall Project at the University of Mississippi
summary: GULFPORT, Miss., June 09, 2026--Tutor Perini Corporation (NYSE: TPC) (the "Company"), a leading civil, building and specialty construction company, announced today that its subsidiary, Roy Anderson Corp, has been awarded a contract valued at approximately $114 million for the Jones Hall Project at the University of Mississippi ("Ole Miss") in Oxford, MS.

headline: Can Verizon's Fiber Expansion Strategy Benefit From Rising AI Demand?
summary: VZ is expanding its fiber footprint following the Frontier acquisition, positioning itself to benefit from growing AI, cloud computing and data center connectivity demand.

headline: AST Rises. Space Stocks Are Volatile Ahead of the SpaceX IPO.
summary: Case in point:  AST SpaceMobile  Shares were up 1.4% in early trading Tuesday, while the  was down 0.3%.  An announcement by the company that three of its communications satellites were slated to head to the stars on June 17, aboard a  SpaceX  rocket, seems to have catalyzed the gains.  AST is building a communications satellite constellation that will be used by  Verizon Communications AT&T  and others to improve wireless service on Earth.

headline: US operators move the goalposts to better frame account and convergence growth
summary: Real, sustainable, long-term growth is decidedly hard to come by these days, and there’s no clearer sign of how unforgiving the US services space has become than the big three nationwide telcos all deciding to overhaul their reporting structures for Q1 2026.

headline: Derq and AT&T Collaborate to Advance AI-Powered Transportation Infrastructure
summary: DETROIT, June 09, 2026--Derq, the intelligent transportation company enabling cities to prevent fatalities using proven, real-world AI at intersections, today announced it is collaborating with AT&T to accelerate the deployment of real-time safety and traffic intelligence across connected transportation ecosystems.

headline: 2 of Wall Street’s Favorite Stocks on Our Watchlist and 1 We Brush Off
summary: The stocks in this article have caught Wall Street’s attention in a big way, with price targets implying returns above 20%. But investors should take these forecasts with a grain of salt because analysts typically say nice things about companies so their firms can win business in other product lines like M&A advisory.

headline: 2 Cash-Heavy Stocks Worth Investigating and 1 We Turn Down
summary: Companies with more cash than debt can be financially resilient, but that doesn’t mean they’re all strong investments. Some lack leverage because they struggle to grow or generate consistent profits, making them unattractive borrowers.
